summary: When catching `requests.HTTPError` in a module where tests patch the entire `requests` module (via `patch("module.requests")`), import the exception class directly — `from requests.exceptions import HTTPError as _RequestsHTTPError` — and use `_RequestsHTTPError` in the `except` clause. Patching `requests` replaces the module object, so `except requests.HTTPError` evaluates to a MagicMock and raises `TypeError: catching classes that do not inherit from BaseException` when any exception is raised in the try block. See `backend/tests/utils/pg_discovery.py`.
